words.meaning: question marker — turns the sentence into a yes/no question

words.note: formal version of 'you' — use with strangers, elders, or anyone you want to show respect to

words.meaning: a respectful honorific placed after a name
words.note: added after someone's name to show respect, like saying 'Mr.' or 'Ms.' but more versatile — used in both formal and polite everyday speech
the plural/formal form of 'is/are' — used here because आप (formal 'you') always takes this form
